cards.title: Cyera targets $12B valuation with $300M funding round
cards.body: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million Series round led by Evolution Equity Partners, achieving an 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses. This valuation reflects strong investor confidence in the security market.

cards.title: Uber cuts AI spending after exhausting budget in months
cards.body: Uber capped employee AI spending after the company had encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible. The spending spree depleted budgets in just four months.

cards.title: Microsoft releases open-source AI evaluation framework
cards.body: Microsoft launched Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing (ASSERT), an open-source framework for spinning up AI evaluations. Developers can now spin up AI behavior tests using simple text descriptions.

cards.title: Martin Scorsese embraces AI for storyboarding
cards.body: The legendary director becomes an unlikely Hollywood advocate for AI technology, though he's using it solely for storyboarding purposes. His adoption signals growing acceptance of AI in creative workflows.

cards.title: Microsoft Scout brings OpenClaw to Microsoft 365
cards.body: Microsoft launched Scout, a new AI assistant inspired by OpenClaw, designed to integrate with the Microsoft 365 system. The tool brings advanced AI capabilities directly into enterprise workflows.

cards.title: Google deploys AI fake call detection technology
cards.body: Google launched fake call detection to protect users against AI deepfake impersonation scams spoofing trusted phone numbers. Scammers are increasingly using AI to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.

cards.title: Microsoft gives developers AI agent behavior control
cards.body: Microsoft released a specification allowing developer, compliance, and security teams to define policies for AI agents in portable policy files. This gives teams portable control over agent behavior across deployments.

cards.title: Amazon faces class action over Ring facial recognition
cards.body: Amazon's Ring faces a class action lawsuit claiming its Familiar Faces feature stores images of passersby without consent. The lawsuit, filed in Seattle, raises privacy concerns around biometric data collection.

cards.title: Trump signs narrower AI oversight executive order
cards.body: President Trump signed a revised AI executive order requiring only voluntary prerelease government reviews of advanced models after industry objections. The order takes a lighter-touch approach than initially proposed.

cards.title: OpenAI launches six new Codex workplace tools
cards.body: OpenAI released six plug-ins targeting specific white-collar jobs: data analytics, creative production, sales, product design, equity investing, and investment banking. Each tool bundles integrations, instructions, and context for specialized work.

cards.title: Anthropic expands Claude Mythos to critical infrastructure
cards.body: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing access to 150 organizations across 15 countries targeting critical infrastructure in power, water, healthcare, and communications. The program protects systems where cyberattacks could affect 100 million people.

cards.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M for AI compliance protection
cards.body: ZeroDrift launched a new AI compliance service that sits between AI models and end users to flag problematic messages. The solution automatically flags and replaces any messages presenting compliance issues.

cards.title: Impulse Space raises $500M to hire human engineers
cards.body: Rocket engine startup Impulse Space raised $500 million prioritizing human talent over AI for engineering complex physical systems. Impulse Space president Eric Romo emphasized that physical systems engineering still depends on human expertise.

cards.title: Alphabet plans $80B investment in AI infrastructure
cards.body: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80 billion to pay for its AI buildout as enterprise and consumer demand exceeds available supply. The company is experiencing strong demand for AI solutions and services.

cards.title: Nvidia pursues $200B CPU market with AI agent PCs
cards.body: Nvidia is chasing the $200 billion CPU market by partnering with Microsoft, Dell, and HP to bring AI agents to consumer PCs. If Nvidia succeeds in making AI agents accessible and useful, it could be transformative.

cards.title: Florida sues OpenAI over violent incident allegations
cards.body: Florida filed a first-of-its-kind lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, partially revolving around a shooting at Florida State University and ChatGPT's alleged role. The lawsuit raises questions about AI platforms' responsibility in violent incidents.

cards.title: SpaceX water needs emerge as IPO risk factor
cards.body: SpaceX identified water access as a risk factor in its IPO filing, stating it needs significant water resources to cool data centers. Abundant, affordable water access is now a critical business challenge for the company.

cards.title: Anthropic files for public stock market debut
cards.body: Anthropic filed to go public as an AI powerhouse with top-tier enterprise customers. The company has evolved from an underdog to a major player in the large language model landscape.

cards.title: WindBorne weather AI outperforms government agencies
cards.body: WindBorne's AI weather startup is out-forecasting government agencies using 400 balloons in flight collecting sensor readings globally. The company's unique combination of model-building and data collection drives its forecasting advances.

cards.title: DuckDuckGo expands no-AI search engine access
cards.body: Alternative search engine DuckDuckGo launched 'no AI' web extensions for Chrome and Firefox as traffic booms. The company makes its no-AI position easier to access for users seeking AI-free search.

cards.title: Microsoft Launches AI Evaluation Framework for Developers
cards.body: Microsoft released Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for AI evaluations. The tool lets developers spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions.

cards.title: Microsoft Scout Brings OpenClaw to Microsoft 365
cards.body: Microsoft launched Scout at Build, a new AI assistant bringing OpenClaw's power to Microsoft 365. The tool is designed to enhance productivity across enterprise workflows.

cards.title: Google Deploys AI Deepfake Detection Against Scams
cards.body: Google rolled out fake call detection to protect users from AI deepfake impersonation scams. The feature combats scammers spoofing trusted numbers and using AI to impersonate authority figures.

cards.title: Microsoft Gives Developers Better AI Agent Control
cards.body: Microsoft released a specification letting developers define policies for AI agents in portable policy files. Compliance and security teams can now better control agent behavior.

cards.title: OpenAI Launches Codex Plug-ins for White-Collar Work
cards.body: OpenAI released six specialized Codex plug-ins for data analytics, creative production, sales, design, investing, and banking. Each tool bundles integrations and instructions to optimize AI for specific jobs.

cards.title: Anthropic Expands Claude Mythos to Critical Infrastructure
cards.body: Anthropic expanded Project Glasswing to 150 organizations across 15+ countries targeting critical infrastructure. The security program now provides AI vulnerability detection in power, water, healthcare, and communications.

cards.title: ZeroDrift Raises $10M for AI Compliance Layer
cards.body: ZeroDrift launched a compliance service that sits between AI models and users to flag problematic messages. The startup protects organizations from AI model failures and policy violations.

cards.title: Alphabet Plans $80B Investment in AI Infrastructure
cards.body: Alphabet announced plans to raise $80B for its AI buildout to meet enterprise demand. The company said demand for AI solutions exceeds its available supply.

cards.title: Nvidia Launches AI Agent PCs with Partners
cards.body: Nvidia partnered with Microsoft, Dell, and HP to launch AI agent-capable PCs. The initiative aims to bring AI agents safely and usefully to mainstream consumers.

cards.title: Florida Sues OpenAI and Sam Altman
cards.body: Florida filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman over violent incidents. The case centers on ChatGPT's alleged role in a shooting at Florida State University.

cards.title: Anthropic Files for Public Offering
cards.body: Anthropic, now an AI powerhouse with top-tier enterprise customers, filed to go public. The company has emerged as a leading LLM developer despite earlier underdog status.

cards.title: AI Weather Startup Outforecasts Government Agencies
cards.body: WindBorne's AI weather model outperforms government forecasts using its unique data collection approach. The company operates 400 balloons globally, gathering sensor readings from 15 launch sites.

cards.title: DuckDuckGo Expands No-AI Search Engine Access
cards.body: DuckDuckGo launched browser extensions for Chrome and Firefox to promote no-AI search. The move comes as traffic to the privacy-focused search engine booms.

cards.title: Small Businesses Leverage AI for Core Operations
cards.body: AI tools now enable small businesses to streamline accounting, design, research, and development. The technology democratizes skills previously requiring dedicated staff.

cards.title: Agentic AI Addresses Global Healthcare Strain
cards.body: Agentic AI systems could help alleviate chronic underinvestment in global healthcare. The technology addresses fragmented care access and high staffing constraints.

cards.title: Pope's Encyclical Frames AI Neutrality Challenge
cards.body: Pope Leo XIV's Magnifica Humanitas encyclical emphasizes that technology is never neutral. The statement calls for courage and solidarity in addressing AI's societal impact.

cards.title: Microsoft Plans AI Agent Operating System
cards.body: Microsoft's Project Solara is an Android OS designed specifically for AI agents instead of traditional apps. The platform represents a shift toward agent-first computing architecture.

cards.title: Mathematicians Warn of AI Threats to Profession
cards.body: Mathematicians are raising concerns as AI industry encroaches on their field. The profession faces challenges from advancing AI capabilities.

cards.title: Android Detects Spoofed Calls and Impersonation
cards.body: Android phones will soon detect spoofed calls and impersonation scams using AI. The feature protects users from deepfake and fraud attacks.

cards.title: GitHub Copilot Users React to New Pricing
cards.body: GitHub Copilot introduced usage-based pricing, drawing reactions from developers. The cost structure reflects growing demand for AI coding assistance.

cards.title: Hackers Exploit Meta's AI Support Chatbot
cards.body: Hackers manipulated Meta's AI support chatbot to steal celebrity Instagram accounts. The breach reveals vulnerabilities in AI customer service systems.
